# Source: Fair Work Ombudsman (fairwork.gov.au)
# Jurisdiction: National workplace relations system, Australia
# Built: 2026-05-08 | NMW effective: 1 July 2025 | SGC: 12% from 1 July 2025
# !! AUDIT USE: Cite file_id and source URL in all formal reports !!
# THIS FILE IS MACHINE-GENERATED — DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY

## [001_employment-conditions] Employment conditions
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/employment-conditions
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:22:00Z
   description: Top-level index of FWO employment conditions topics
   topics_covered: ['awards', 'agreements', 'NES', 'public holidays', 'hours of work', 'contracts', 'flexibility', 'protections', 'discrimination', 'workers compensation']
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth)

## [002_awards__about-awards] About awards
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/employment-conditions/awards/about-awards
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:22:00Z
   award_definition: A Modern Award is a legal document outlining minimum pay rates and conditions of employment
   award_types: ['industry awards', 'occupational awards', 'industry and occupational awards', 'enterprise awards', 'state reference public sector awards']
   award_overridden_by: Enterprise agreement (while agreement is in force)
   coverage_clause_location: Usually clause 4 of each award
   contract_cannot_undercut_award: True
   dispute_resolution_required_in_awards: True
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth)

## [003_awards__award-classifications] Award classifications
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/employment-conditions/awards/award-classifications
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:23:00Z
   classification_determines: Minimum pay rate for the employee
   classification_found_in: Schedule to each Modern Award
   employer_must_classify_correctly: True
   misclassification_risk: Underpayment — employee entitled to higher rate
   job_title_does_not_determine_classification: True
   duties_and_responsibilities_determine_classification: True
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth); individual Modern Award classification schedules

## [004_awards__award-and-agreement-free] Award and agreement free wages and conditions
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/employment-conditions/awards/award-and-agreement-free-wages-and-conditions
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:24:00Z
   award_agreement_free_minimum: National Minimum Wage ($24.95/hr from 1 July 2025)
   award_agreement_free_also_entitled_to: National Employment Standards (NES)
   most_employees_covered_by_award: True
   check_coverage_via: Pay and Conditions Tool (fairwork.gov.au/pay-and-wages/pay-calculator)
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth)

## [005_nes__index] National Employment Standards
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/employment-conditions/national-employment-standards
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:25:00Z
   nes_count: 11
   nes_entitlements: ['Maximum weekly hours (38 hours + reasonable additional)', 'Flexible working arrangements', 'Parental leave and related entitlements', 'Annual leave (4 weeks/year full-time)', "Personal/carer's leave and compassionate leave", 'Community service leave', 'Long service leave', 'Public holidays', 'Notice of termination and redundancy pay', 'Fair Work Information Statement', 'Casual Employment Information Statement (casuals)']
   nes_applies_to: All employees covered by the national workplace relations system
   cannot_be_excluded_by_contract_or_award: True
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) Part 2-2 (ss.61–131)

## [006_nes__maximum-weekly-hours] Maximum weekly hours fact sheet
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/tools-and-resources/fact-sheets/minimum-workplace-entitlements/maximum-weekly-hours
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:26:00Z
   maximum_ordinary_hours_full_time: 38
   maximum_ordinary_hours_part_time: As per their agreement (not more than 38)
   additional_hours_permitted: Reasonable additional hours only
   employee_can_refuse_unreasonable_overtime: True
   factors_for_reasonableness: ['risk to health and safety', 'personal circumstances', 'notice given', 'nature of work', 'usual patterns of work']
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) s.62

## [007_hours-of-work__index] Hours of work, breaks and rosters
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/employment-conditions/hours-of-work-breaks-and-rosters
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:30:00Z
   ordinary_hours_set_by: Award, enterprise agreement, or employment contract
   spread_of_hours_definition: Times of day during which ordinary hours can be worked
   rostered_days_off_rdo_applies: Where award or agreement provides for them
   overtime_trigger: Work outside ordinary hours as defined in award/agreement
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth)

## [008_hours-of-work__overtime] When overtime applies
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/employment-conditions/hours-of-work-breaks-and-rosters/hours-of-work/when-overtime-applies
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:31:00Z
   overtime_definition: Work performed OUTSIDE ordinary hours set by award, agreement, or contract
   overtime_triggers: ['beyond maximum daily ordinary hours', 'beyond weekly ordinary hours', 'outside part-time agreed hours', 'outside spread of hours']
   employee_can_refuse_unreasonable_overtime: True
   employer_can_require_overtime_only_if: Reasonable (per award/agreement)
   fatigue_and_safety_must_be_considered: True
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) s.62

## [009_hours-of-work__breaks] Breaks
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/employment-conditions/hours-of-work-breaks-and-rosters/breaks
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:31:00Z
   break_types: ['rest break (rest pause / tea break)', 'meal break', 'crib break (paid meal break)', 'break between shifts']
   break_rules_set_by: Award, enterprise agreement, or registered agreement
   crib_break_is_paid: True
   crib_break_applies_when: Employee may need to resume work during meal break or cannot leave work area
   minimum_break_between_shifts: Varies by award — check applicable award
   legislation: Award/enterprise agreement specific; Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth)

## [010_leave__annual-leave] Annual leave
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/leave/annual-leave
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:32:00Z
   annual_leave_weeks_per_year_full_time: 4
   shiftworkers_entitlement_weeks: 5
   casual_employees_entitled: False
   accrual: Progressive throughout the year
   accumulates_if_unused: True
   paid_at: Ordinary rate of pay (+ leave loading if award provides)
   leave_loading_common_rate_percent: 17.5
   direction_to_take_leave: Allowed under some awards when excess leave accrued
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) ss.87–93

## [011_leave__sick-and-carers-leave] Sick and carer's leave
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/leave/sick-and-carers-leave
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:33:00Z
   sick_leave_days_per_year_full_time: 10
   sick_leave_accrual: Progressive, accumulates year to year if unused
   paid_personal_leave_entitlement: 10 days per year (for personal illness or caring responsibilities)
   casual_employees_paid_personal_leave: False
   casual_unpaid_carers_leave_days_per_occasion: 2
   compassionate_leave_days_per_occasion: 2
   compassionate_leave_paid_for_permanent: True
   notice_required: As soon as practicable
   evidence_employer_can_request: True
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) ss.96–107

## [012_leave__long-service-leave] Long service leave
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/leave/long-service-leave
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:33:00Z
   long_service_leave_set_by: State and Territory legislation (not the Fair Work Act)
   general_qualifying_period_years: Approximately 7–10 years depending on state/territory
   entitlement_varies_by: State or territory where employee works
   portable_schemes_exist: True
   portable_scheme_industries: ['building and construction', 'contract cleaning', 'community services (some states)']
   pro_rata_on_death_or_genuine_redundancy: True
   legislation: State/Territory Long Service Leave Acts (varies by jurisdiction)

## [013_pay__superannuation] Tax and superannuation
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/pay-and-wages/tax-and-superannuation
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T08:34:00Z
   superannuation_guarantee_rate_2025_26: 11.5
   sgc_rate_from_2025_07_01: 12.0
   sgc_rate_note: SGC increases to 12% from 1 July 2025
   ote_definition: Ordinary Time Earnings — the base on which SGC is calculated
   sgc_calculation_base: Ordinary Time Earnings (OTE)
   ote_includes: ['ordinary hours earnings', 'over-award payments', 'allowances', 'annual leave pay', 'sick leave pay']
   ote_excludes: ['overtime pay (generally)', 'genuine redundancy payments', 'reimbursements']
   payment_due_quarterly: True
   quarterly_payment_dates: ['28 October', '28 January', '28 April', '28 July']
   choice_of_fund: Employees generally entitled to choose their super fund
   stapled_super_fund: Employer must check ATO for employee's stapled fund if no choice made
   minimum_monthly_earnings_threshold_abolished: 1 July 2022 — super now payable regardless of earnings
   legislation: Superannuation Guarantee (Administration) Act 1992 (Cth); Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) s.68 (NES)

## [014_pay__minimum-wages] Minimum wages
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/pay-and-wages/minimum-wages
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T09:00:00+00:00
   national_minimum_wage_hourly: 24.95
   national_minimum_wage_weekly: 948.0
   currency: AUD
   effective_date: 2026-07-01
   wage_increase_percentage: 3.5
   based_on_hours_per_week: 38
   applies_to: Employees not covered by an award or registered agreement

## [015_pay__penalty-rates] Penalty rates
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/pay-and-wages/penalty-rates-allowances-and-other-payments/penalty-rates
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T09:00:00+00:00
   trigger_scenarios: ['weekends', 'public holidays', 'overtime', 'late nights', 'early mornings']
   determined_by: Award or registered agreement
   casual_loading_interaction: Both casual loading and penalty rate calculated on the base minimum pay rate
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth)

## [016_pay__overtime] Overtime pay
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/pay-and-wages/penalty-rates-allowances-and-other-payments/overtime-pay
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T09:00:00+00:00
   ordinary_weekly_hours_full_time: 38
   common_overtime_rate_first_hours: 150% (time and a half)
   common_overtime_rate_extended: 200% (double time)
   toil_available: True
   toil_requires_written_agreement: True
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) s.62

## [017_pay__payslips] Pay slips
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/pay-and-wages/paying-wages/pay-slips
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T09:00:00+00:00
   must_be_provided_within: 1 business day of payment
   format: ['Electronic', 'Hard copy']
   must_be_in_english: True
   family_dv_leave_on_payslip: False
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth); Fair Work Regulations 2009

## [018_pay__record-keeping] Record-keeping
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/pay-and-wages/paying-wages/record-keeping
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T09:00:00+00:00
   retention_period_years: 7
   format: Legible, English, readily accessible
   false_records_prohibited: True
   inspector_access: At any time
   reverse_onus_risk: Absence of records may shift burden to employer in underpayment disputes
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth); Fair Work Regulations 2009 reg.3.31–3.46

   casual_loading_standard_percent: 25
   new_definition_effective_date: 2026-08-26
   employee_choice_pathway_effective: 2026-02-26
   ceis_required: True
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth), as amended by Fair Work Legislation Amendment (Closing Loopholes) Acts 2023–2024

   pay_secrecy_enforceable: False
   pay_secrecy_change_date: 2022-12-07
   fixed_term_max_duration_years: 2
   fixed_term_restriction_date: 2023-12-06
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth)

   age_45_plus_extra_notice_weeks: 1
   age_45_plus_minimum_service_years: 2
   sick_leave_paid_out_on_termination: False
   annual_leave_paid_out_on_termination: True

## [022_ending__redundancy] Redundancy
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/ending-employment/redundancy
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T09:00:00+00:00
   small_business_threshold_employees: 15
   small_business_redundancy_pay_entitlement: False
   casual_redundancy_pay: False
   10_year_reduction_note: Redundancy pay reduces from 16 to 12 weeks at 10+ years — consistent with long service leave entitlements
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) s.119–123

## [023_ending__unfair-dismissal] Unfair dismissal
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/ending-employment/unfair-dismissal
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T09:00:00+00:00
   claim_lodged_with: Fair Work Commission (FWC) — not Fair Work Ombudsman
   application_deadline_days: 21
   minimum_employment_period_standard_months: 6
   minimum_employment_period_small_business_months: 12
   small_business_threshold_employees: 15
   high_income_threshold_2024_25: 175000
   max_compensation_weeks_pay: 26
   preferred_remedy: Reinstatement
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) ss.385–392

## [024_conditions__public-holidays] Public holidays
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/employment-conditions/public-holidays
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T09:00:00+00:00
   right_to_absent: True
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) s.114–116
   employer_can_request_work: True
   employee_can_refuse_unreasonable_request: True
   typical_penalty_rate_range: 225%–250% of ordinary rate
   part_time_entitlement: Only on days they are ordinarily rostered to work
   casual_entitlement: Only if ordinarily rostered for that day

   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/leave/parental-leave
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T09:00:00+00:00
   eligibility_minimum_service_months: 12
   casual_eligible: True
   casual_requires_regular_systematic: True
   unpaid_leave_initial_months: 12
   unpaid_leave_maximum_months: 24
   notice_required_weeks: 10
   right_to_return_protected: True
   dismissal_on_parental_leave_prohibited: True
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) ss.67–85

## [026_leave__family-domestic-violence] Family and domestic violence leave
   SOURCE: https://www.fairwork.gov.au/leave/family-and-domestic-violence-leave
   SCRAPED: 2026-05-08T09:00:00+00:00
   days_per_year: 10
   paid: True
   accumulates_from_day_1: True
   does_not_roll_over_if_unused: True
   casual_employees_entitled: True
   payslip_must_not_mention: True
   effective_date_all_employees: 2023-08-01
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) ss.106A–106P

   right_to_refuse_contact: True
   adverse_action_prohibited: True
   dispute_resolution_body: Fair Work Commission (FWC)
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) ss.333M–333T (inserted by Closing Loopholes Act 2024)

   boot_required: True
   nes_cannot_be_excluded: True
   requires_majority_employee_vote: True

   ifa_notice_to_terminate_days: 28
   legislation: Fair Work Act 2009 (Cth) Part 2-4

   set_by: Modern Award or enterprise agreement
   must_appear_on_payslip: True

================================================================================
FILE: 031_tax__resident-income-tax-rates.json
SOURCE: Australian Taxation Office (ATO)
URL: https://www.ato.gov.au/tax-rates-and-codes/tax-rates-australian-residents
ATO LAST UPDATED: 18 June 2025
CORPUS CAPTURED: 2026-05-09
YEARS COVERED: 2007-08 to 2025-26 (19 income years)
================================================================================

CURRENT YEAR: 2025-26
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Bracket 1: $0 – $18,200          → Nil (tax-free threshold)
Bracket 2: $18,201 – $45,000     → 16c per $1 over $18,200
Bracket 3: $45,001 – $135,000    → $4,288 + 30c per $1 over $45,000
Bracket 4: $135,001 – $190,000   → $31,288 + 37c per $1 over $135,000
Bracket 5: $190,001+             → $51,638 + 45c per $1 over $190,000

PREVIOUS YEAR (2024-25): IDENTICAL to 2025-26 (Stage 3 cuts schedule unchanged)

PRE-STAGE-3 (2023-24 and earlier):
Bracket 2: $18,201 – $45,000     → 19c per $1 over $18,200  [was 16c]
Bracket 3: $45,001 – $120,000    → $5,092 + 32.5c           [different thresholds]
Bracket 4: $120,001 – $180,000   → $29,467 + 37c
Bracket 5: $180,001+             → $51,667 + 45c

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- Rates EXCLUDE Medicare levy (add 2% for most taxpayers)
- Rates EXCLUDE Medicare levy surcharge
- Low Income Tax Offset (LITO) 2025-26: max $700, full offset ≤ $37,500,
  phases out at 5c/$1 to $45,000, then 1.5c/$1 to $66,667
- Tax-free threshold was $6,000 pre-2012-13; became $18,200 from 2012-13
- Formula: tax = base_tax + marginal_rate_pct × (income − bracket_from + 1)

PAYG WITHHOLDING NOTE:
- Employers use ATO withholding schedules (NAT 1008/3539), not these brackets directly
- These rates used for end-of-year tax return calculations
- PAYG withholding reasonableness in audits: compare annual PAYG withheld against
  expected tax at these marginal rates for the employee's income band
